pages.text: $(bold)Stress$() is Create's power budget. Every $(create)source$() supplies a pool of $(bold)Stress Units (SU)$(); every machine $(italic)consumes$() some while it runs. Spend more than you make and the network is $(bold)overstressed$() — everything grinds to a halt.$(br2)A machine's stress cost scales with $(italic)speed$(): the same press costs twice as much spun twice as fast.
pages.title: Stress

pages.text: $(bold)Reading it.$() Put on the $(create)Engineer's Goggles$() and look at any shaft: you'll see capacity used versus available. A $(bold)Stressometer$() gives a precise gauge.$(br2)When you overstress, you have three choices: add a bigger source, run machines slower, or split the work across more networks.
pages.title: Reading Stress
